title: Sorter and WCS tote exchange operating concept
summary: The WCS coordinates tote removal and replacement with the sorter PLC when a tote reaches configured fill criteria.
details: According to the source, parcels are directed into totes after the sorter starts. When a tote reaches the customer-specified weight, parcel count, or volume, the WCS sends a message to the sorter PLC to stop diverting packages. The WCS then triggers an AGV to retrieve the full tote, and another AGV places an empty tote in the vacated position. After replacement, the WCS sends a message to the sorter PLC to resume sortation.

title: Tipper and bagging flow reference
summary: AGVs use floor QR codes to route totes to open tippers, where the tote is sensed, pulled onto the tipper, and emptied into a parcel bag through a chute.
details: The source states that specifically placed floor QR codes direct the AGV carrying a tote to an open tipper. When the tipper gripper senses the tote, it pulls the tote onto the tipper. The tipper deposits the parcel into a parcel bag via a chute. A bag ID label is generated and placed on the bag by the bagging employee, and the bag is then placed on a take-away conveyor. After the tipping cycle is complete and the tote is empty, the WCS indicates to the AGV that the task is complete and the empty tote is removed.

title: Hospital station exception handling reference
summary: The hospital station is used for exception totes associated with mis-sort, bad tip, or tote overload conditions.
details: The source explains that in the event of a mis-sort, bad tip, or tote overload, the WCS directs the AGV to take the tote to the right side of the hospital station for operator handling. The AGV then moves to the left side of the hospital station to pick up an empty tote and re-enters the sorter area to await further instructions.

title: System API control functions and meanings
summary: The manual defines the named controls in the System API Controls area and describes what each control does at a high level.
details: Table 4-1 describes the controls as follows: Get System State displays the current state of OptiSweep. System Startup allows the user to put the system in a running state. System Shutdown shuts down the system and sends AGVs to a shutdown queue. System Close Out sends all totes to the tippers to close out the sorter. System Purge sends all totes to the tippers and labels are not printed during a purge. Reset Metrics resets the metrics.

title: Operational timing notes for shutdown, close out, purge, and metrics reset
summary: The manual includes timing and usage notes for several system-level controls, indicating when they should or should not be used.

title: Tipper service interfaces and connection points
summary: The tipper assembly is connected through electrical, network, pneumatic, and mechanical interfaces that are explicitly identified in the replacement section.
details: The source identifies several service interfaces associated with the tipper: a power cable using a 6-pin Molex connector, an Ethernet cable connected to the ClearLink controller, an air line with a quick disconnect at the bottom of the column, and conduit bringing power, air, and Ethernet to the column. Mechanically, the tipper is mounted to a sub-plate using four hex head screws. Access to the motor may require removal of a station side panel secured by nine self-tapping fasteners.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The section names the power cable (6-pin Molex), Ethernet to the ClearLink controller, air line quick disconnect, conduit to the column, and four screws holding the tipper to the sub-plate.

retrieval_text: Tipper component reference for service interfaces. The tipper connects to a control box and column through a 6-pin Molex power cable, Ethernet to a ClearLink controller, an air line quick disconnect at the bottom of the column, and conduit carrying power, air, and Ethernet. The tipper mounts to a sub-plate with four hex head screws.

title: Safety and contamination controls for tipper replacement
summary: The replacement section specifies lockout/tagout, PPE, supported lifting, and air-line contamination prevention considerations for tipper service.
details: The source requires lockout/tagout of the tipper before removal work begins and lists PPE as safety glasses and gloves. It also states that the tipper should be supported when the four mounting screws are removed. For the pneumatic connection, the quick disconnect is left with the tipper side of the air line, and tape is added over the air line and quick disconnect to prevent debris from entering either side of the air line.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Required PPE includes safety glasses and gloves; LOTO the tipper; support the tipper during removal; tape over the air line and quick disconnect to prevent debris entry.

retrieval_text: Safety reference for tipper replacement. The manual specifies lockout/tagout before service, PPE of safety glasses and gloves, supporting the tipper during removal from the sub-plate, and covering the air line and quick disconnect with tape to prevent debris contamination.

title: Related components referenced during tipper replacement
summary: The tipper replacement section references other removable or associated components that may be involved in access or reassembly.
details: The source notes that removal of the gripper and counter-balance weights is optional but recommended during tipper replacement. It also references the station side panel for access to the motor and states that top and/or bottom side panels may need to be reinstalled after replacement. These references indicate that the tipper is serviced in relation to surrounding access panels and attached handling components.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Optional but recommended removal of the gripper and counter-balance weights is referenced, and side panels may be removed for access and reinstalled afterward.

retrieval_text: Component reference for related assemblies in tipper replacement. The manual associates tipper service with the gripper, counter-balance weights, station side panel, and top or bottom side panels. These components may be removed for access or reinstalled after the new tipper is mounted.

title: Tipper commissioning overview
summary: Tipper commissioning is required when any tipper component is replaced so the affected tipper can be aligned with the other tipper and the grippers and axes can be leveled.
details: The source describes tipper commissioning as a multi-part setup activity performed after replacement of any tipper component. The stated purpose is alignment with the other tipper and ensuring the grippers and axes are level. The outlined commissioning scope includes applying power, setting controller parameters, setting the IP address, checking I/O connections, confirming the end effector is plumb, setting Z-axis home, leveling the end effector on both tippers, checking motor-to-frame clearance, setting tote pickup position to 16 3/4 in., setting tote dump position, loading firmware, and validating setup.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: When any tipper component is replaced, that tipper needs to be aligned with the other tipper to ensure the grippers and axes are level. The following is an outline of the commissioning process.

retrieval_text: Tipper commissioning overview after component replacement. Purpose is to align the replaced tipper with the other tipper and ensure grippers and axes are level. Scope includes power, controller parameters, IP address, I/O checks, end effector plumb, Z-axis home, leveling, collision clearance, tote pickup and dump positions, firmware load, and setup validation.

title: Tipper commissioning resource requirements
summary: The manual specifies the tools, staffing, and estimated duration associated with tipper commissioning.
details: For tipper commissioning, the listed tools are a level, digital measure, large square, and screw driver. The source states that two people are required and the time required is three hours.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Tools Needed: Level, digital measure, large square, screw driver. Personnel Required: Two people. Time Required: Three hours.

retrieval_text: Tipper commissioning resource requirements: tools are level, digital measure, large square, and screw driver; personnel required is two people; time required is three hours.

title: Station power control reference for tipper commissioning
summary: The commissioning section identifies the electrical panel power switch as the station power control used at the start of tipper commissioning.
details: Within the tipper commissioning section, the first subtopic is applying power to the station. The source identifies the power switch on the electrical panel and states that it is turned to the "On" position when applying power to the station.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Apply Power to Station: Turn the power switch on the electrical panel to the "On" position.

retrieval_text: Reference for station power control during tipper commissioning: the electrical panel contains the power switch used to apply power to the station, with an On position identified in the manual.

title: Visu_MDs HMI screen reference for controller parameter setup
summary: The operator station HMI screen named "Visu_MDs" is referenced as the screen used when setting initial controller parameters for each tipper axis.
details: In the controller parameter setup subsection, the source states that initial parameters are set for each axis of the tippers. It identifies the operator station HMI and directs navigation to the "Visu_MDs" screen, labeled with function key F2. The source does not provide additional screen fields or parameter names in this excerpt.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: On the operator station HMI, navigate to the "Visu_MDs" screen (F2). Set the initial parameters for each axis of the tippers.

retrieval_text: HMI screen reference: Visu_MDs on the operator station HMI, accessed with F2, is used in the controller parameter setup context for each tipper axis.

title: Access role requirement for changing tipper controller settings
summary: Changing settings in the controller parameter setup context requires a maintenance or admin login on the operator station HMI.
details: A note in the controller parameter subsection states that the user must be logged in as maintenance or admin to be able to change the settings. This is presented as an access requirement tied to modifying settings from the HMI during tipper commissioning.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: NOTE: You must be logged in as maintenance or admin to be able to change the settings.

retrieval_text: Access requirement for tipper controller setting changes: operator must be logged in as maintenance or admin on the operator station HMI to change settings.

title: Replacement scanner setup overview
summary: The manual states that when a scanner is replaced, the new scanner must be set up using a laptop-based Barcode Interface application and connectivity to both AGV control and Hospital control.
details: Section 7.3.24 describes setup for a replacement scanner. The stated requirements include a Windows 10 or 11 laptop with "Barcode Interface.exe" installed, a Zebra DS3608-SR scanner dock, USB cable, power adapter, two 120 VAC outlets, and network connectivity from the laptop to the AGV and Hospital control systems. The source says connectivity should be validated by pinging their IP addresses.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: If a scanner is replaced, the new scanner needs to be setup. Requirements include a Windows 10 or 11 laptop with Barcode Interface.exe, Zebra DS3608-SR scanner dock, USB cable, power adapter, 120 VAC outlets, and network connectivity to the AGV and Hospital control.

retrieval_text: Replacement scanner setup in OptiSweep uses Barcode Interface.exe on a Windows 10 or 11 laptop, a Zebra DS3608-SR scanner dock, USB cable, power adapter, two 120 VAC outlets, and laptop network connectivity to AGV control and Hospital control validated by ping.

title: Scanner dock and battery charge status indicators
summary: The scanner setup section describes expected dock power indication and a battery charge indicator on the scanner.
details: When the scanner dock, USB cable, and auxiliary power are connected and the adapter is plugged into a 120 VAC outlet, the source says the dock should light up. A note adds that if the scanner is uncharged, it must rest on the dock until the red battery symbol shown on top turns green.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The dock should light up. If the scanner is uncharged it will need to rest on the dock until the red battery symbol shown on top turns green.

retrieval_text: Scanner setup status indicators include the dock lighting up when powered and the scanner battery symbol changing from red to green while charging on the dock.

title: Barcode Interface connectivity indicator and calibration reference
summary: The Barcode Interface screen includes a connectivity indication at the bottom right, with green and red states referenced in the manual.
details: The source instructs the user to check connectivity on the bottom right of the Barcode Interface screen. It states that if the indicator is green, no further action is needed for that check. If it is red, the screen provides a "Calibrate Scanner" option and onscreen instructions. This establishes a screen-level connectivity status reference and the presence of a calibration function.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Check connectivity on the bottom right of the screen. If green, skip this step. If red, click "Calibrate Scanner" and follow the onscreen instructions.

retrieval_text: Barcode Interface has a connectivity indicator at the bottom right of the screen. The manual references green and red states and a Calibrate Scanner option when the indicator is red.

title: Scanner settings access and default password reference
summary: The scanner setup section references a settings button in the application and states a default password value for accessing settings.
details: The manual says settings are checked by clicking the settings button and supplying the password. It explicitly states that the default password is "Opti". The source also notes that the laptop login uses a FORTNA account before launching the Barcode Interface application.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Log into the laptop with a FORTNA account. Launch the Barcode Interface application.
Check the settings by clicking the settings button, supply the password. By default, the password is “Opti”.

retrieval_text: Scanner settings in Barcode Interface are accessed through a settings button. The manual states the default password is Opti and references logging into the laptop with a FORTNA account.

title: Scanner prefix and endpoint configuration reference
summary: The scanner configuration includes prefix values that must match tote and location labels, plus HTTP and OPC URL settings tied to AGV control and Hospital control IP addresses.
details: The source says scanner settings should be verified so that prefixes match the labels on totes and locations. It also states that both the HTTP and OPC settings contain URLs that should be changed to include the AGV control and Hospital control IP addresses respectively. The settings are then saved with a "Save Settings" button.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Verify prefixes match the labels on the totes and locations. Change the URL for both the HTTP and OPC setting to contain the AGV control and Hospital control IP addresses respectively. Save the settings with the "Save Settings" button.

retrieval_text: Scanner configuration includes prefixes that match tote and location labels and HTTP and OPC URL settings that contain AGV control and Hospital control IP addresses, with a Save Settings button used to save configuration.
